top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging configured for the library publishing pipeline
top_gaps.impact: No verifiable audit trail for published npm packages; supply chain risk

top_gaps.title: Centralized Identity Integration
top_gaps.impact: Contributor access managed solely through GitHub native controls

top_gaps.title: API Authentication
top_gaps.gap: npm publishing uses static token rather than OIDC-based provenance
top_gaps.impact: Long-lived token increases supply chain attack surface

top_gaps.title: API Versioning Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No explicit in-API versioning mechanism beyond semver
top_gaps.impact: Configuration schema changes lack versioned migration paths

top_gaps.title: Service Discovery
top_gaps.gap: No formal API catalog or machine-readable plugin registry
top_gaps.impact: Plugin ecosystem relies on manual discovery rather than automated cataloging

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, TESTING_DOCS.md, 80+ examples, JSON schemas)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Knowledge agent that indexes webpack documentation, examples, and schemas to answer developer configuration questions
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate OpenAPI-style description from TypeScript declarations. Index examples/ as searchable recipes.

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (9 GitHub Actions workflows covering lint, test, release, benchmarks)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent that monitors CI status, triggers benchmark comparisons, manages release workflows, and auto-triages failing tests
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose GitHub Actions API as agent-callable tools. Define trigger conditions for benchmark regression alerts.

findings.title: No Audit Logging for Publishing Pipeline
findings.description: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging is configured. The npm publishing process in .github/workflows/release.yml has no provenance attestation or immutable audit trail for published packages.
findings.gap: No audit logging for the npm publishing process, dependency supply chain events, or contributor access changes beyond GitHub's native capabilities. No immutable log storage for release artifacts.
findings.recommendation: Implement npm provenance/Sigstore artifact signing to create an immutable, verifiable audit trail for published packages. Consider enabling GitHub audit log streaming.

findings.title: No Centralized Identity Integration
findings.description: No centralized identity provider integration exists. Contributor access is managed solely through GitHub organization settings with no SSO enforcement visible from repository configuration.
findings.gap: No centralized IdP for the development workflow. No SSO enforcement visible for the webpack GitHub organization.
findings.recommendation: For an open-source library, GitHub's native identity management is standard practice. If the organization has enterprise requirements, consider GitHub Enterprise with SAML SSO enforcement.

findings.title: Static Token Authentication for Publishing
findings.description: The npm publishing process uses static NPM_TOKEN authentication rather than OIDC-based provenance publishing. The ATX transform workflow demonstrates the better OIDC pattern but the release workflow does not follow it.
findings.gap: npm publishing relies on static token authentication rather than OIDC-based provenance publishing. No npm provenance attestation is configured.
findings.recommendation: Enable npm provenance publishing (--provenance flag) which uses OIDC tokens from GitHub Actions to cryptographically attest package origin, eliminating long-lived NPM_TOKEN secrets.

findings.title: Limited Vulnerability Scanning
findings.description: Dependabot is configured for automated dependency updates and GitHub-hosted runners are automatically patched. However, no explicit vulnerability scanning of production dependencies exists beyond Dependabot's default alerting, and no supply chain attestation (SLSA) is implemented.
findings.gap: No explicit vulnerability scanning of production dependencies beyond Dependabot alerts. No SLSA provenance for build process.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit npm audit step to CI pipeline. Consider implementing SLSA Build Level 3 provenance for published artifacts.

findings.title: No Explicit API Versioning Strategy
findings.description: Webpack uses semantic versioning for npm packages and Changesets for release management, but has no explicit in-API versioning mechanism. JSON schemas in schemas/ define configuration structure but are not versioned independently.
findings.gap: No formal API versioning strategy beyond semver. Schema definitions are not independently versioned. Deprecated options rely on runtime warnings rather than a structured deprecation lifecycle.
findings.recommendation: Implement versioned schema definitions (e.g., WebpackOptions.v5.json) to enable tooling to validate configurations against specific schema versions. Document the deprecation lifecycle with timeline commitments.

findings.title: No Formal API Catalog or Plugin Registry
findings.description: The webpack API is documented through TypeScript declarations and JSON schemas but there is no formal API catalog, no machine-readable plugin registry, and no discovery mechanism for the loader/plugin ecosystem beyond npm search.
findings.gap: No formal API catalog or plugin registry. Ecosystem relies on npm search and documentation rather than automated discovery.
findings.recommendation: Generate an API catalog from TypeScript declarations (e.g., using TypeDoc or API Extractor). A machine-readable plugin interface specification would enable tooling to discover and validate plugin compatibility.

findings.title: JavaScript with Legacy Node.js Support
findings.description: JavaScript (CommonJS) with JSDoc type annotations and TypeScript for type checking. Modern tooling (Jest 30, ESLint 9, TypeScript 5.9) but minimum Node.js support at 10.x is far below current LTS.
findings.gap: Minimum Node.js 10.x support prevents adoption of modern Node.js APIs. JavaScript source rather than TypeScript source relies on JSDoc for type safety.
findings.recommendation: Raise minimum Node.js version to 18.x or 20.x in next major version. Consider migrating to TypeScript source for stronger type guarantees.

findings.title: Filesystem-Only Persistent Cache
findings.description: Webpack's persistent cache (lib/cache/) stores compilation artifacts in the local filesystem. No option for remote/managed storage backends for distributed build environments.
findings.gap: Persistent cache is filesystem-only. Cache sharing across CI build agents requires external tooling.
findings.recommendation: Provide a cache backend plugin interface allowing consumers to implement S3-backed or other remote cache strategies for distributed builds.

findings.title: Minor Filesystem Abstraction Gaps
findings.description: Well-structured data access layer with inputFileSystem/outputFileSystem abstractions. Some internal modules access fs directly rather than through the compiler's filesystem abstraction.
findings.gap: Some internal modules bypass the compiler's filesystem abstraction. Cache layer has separate filesystem access path.
findings.recommendation: Route all filesystem operations through the compiler's inputFileSystem/outputFileSystem for consistency and to enable alternative storage backends.

findings.title: Secrets Without Rotation Policy
findings.description: All secrets managed through GitHub Actions secrets with no hardcoded credentials in source. OIDC used for AWS access. However, no automated rotation configured for long-lived tokens (CODECOV_TOKEN, DISCORD_WEBHOOK, BOT keys, CODSPEED_TOKEN).
findings.gap: GitHub Actions secrets provide encrypted storage but no automated rotation. No documented rotation cadence.
findings.recommendation: Implement token rotation schedules. Where possible, migrate to short-lived OIDC tokens. Document secret inventory and rotation cadence.

findings.title: No Dedicated SAST Tool
findings.description: CI pipeline includes Dependabot, dependency-review (license focus), ESLint, and Codecov enforcement. GitHub Actions pinned by SHA. But no dedicated SAST tool and no explicit npm audit step.
findings.gap: No dedicated SAST tool (CodeQL, Semgrep). Dependency-review focuses on license compliance not vulnerability detection. No explicit npm audit CI step.
findings.recommendation: Add GitHub CodeQL or Semgrep for JavaScript SAST. Add npm audit --audit-level=high step. Consider OpenSSF Scorecard monitoring.
